nodes.label: Kando
nodes.description: Kando is a pie-menu launcher application for Linux. The screenshot shows its menu configuration UI titled 'All Menus'.

nodes.label: Primary Menu
nodes.description: A menu entry in Kando named 'Primary' with shortcut Shift+Meta+F1. It is configured with Hover Mode enabled under Menu Behavior, and supports Menu Conditions like Limit to Specific Apps, Specific Window Titles, and Specific Screen Area.

nodes.label: Kando Pie Menu UI
nodes.description: Visual pie menu interface in the center of the Kando configuration screen showing directional slots (up, down, left, right) around a central hub, with an 'Add Menu Items' button at the bottom.

nodes.label: kando-add-new-menu.png
nodes.description: Screenshot of Kando's 'All Menus' configuration panel showing the Primary menu entry selected, its pie menu preview in the center, and configuration settings (shortcut, tags, menu behavior, menu conditions) on the right panel. A red arrow highlights the Primary menu entry in the left sidebar.
